topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is a collection of various materials and tools used by System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: What type of license does the Book of Secret Knowledge repository use?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository uses the MIT License.

entries.q: What is the target audience for the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The target audience for the Book of Secret Knowledge repository includes System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: How can users contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository by making pull requests and adding valid and well-reasoned explanations about their changes or comments.

entries.q: What is the RSS/Atom feed used for in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The RSS/Atom feed is used to keep users informed about all changes made to the repository.

entries.q: What are some of the main chapters in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The main chapters include CLI Tools, GUI Tools, Web Tools, Systems/Services, Networks, Containers/Orchestration, and more.

entries.q: What are some examples of shells included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Examples of shells included are GNU Bash, Zsh, and tclsh.

entries.q: What are some examples of shell plugins included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Examples of shell plugins included are z, fzf, zsh-autosuggestions, and zsh-syntax-highlighting.
